points.speaker: Canfield
points.text: SRO program proactively initiated placement of naloxone (Narcan) in all schools from elementary through high school during county-wide shortage
points.source: Canfield : So just to add to that, it was the SRR program that initiated proactively getting Narcan into the schools. And so that's from the high school, middle school into the elementary school especially during a shortage county wide.

points.speaker: Unidentified 2
points.text: Of roughly 200 seized vaping devices, approximately 50 were donated to Stanford for research; the remainder were kept as evidence per MVPD protocols and subsequently destroyed if not needed
points.source: Unidentified 2 : We talked about the vaping devices and you said 50th of the 200 were donated to Stanford... What happened to the other hundred and fifty... Canfield : Subsequently destroyed once we're done if they're not needed for a case.

decisions.text: Staff will clearly name all subcommittees in work plan report during October meeting subcommittee appointments
decisions.source: Rampart : And that would be a perfect time to, in that report, to be clear about the names of all the subcommittees.

original_point.text: Board members requested clearer formal naming of subcommittees in work plan, particularly regarding traffic stop/RIPA subcommittee designation
original_point.source: Unidentified 1 : So there's our request to clearly name those in the current work plan and to just clarify that information.
original_point.speaker: Unidentified 1

rejected_items.explanation: This point is redundant with the decision already validated about staff clearly naming subcommittees. The transcript shows this was a request that led to Rampart's commitment about clarifying subcommittee names in the October report. The substance is already captured in the decision item.

validation_details.reasoning: GT1 and GT2 describe the same Narcan initiative but with different focus and attribution. GT2 provides cleaner, more accurate information with correct speaker attribution. The vaping device disposal is a separate topic that should be kept as its own point.

Board request point was appropriately excluded as redundant with validated decision.
